title: ML coding from scratch — k-means, kNN, logistic regression
focus: Implement, time, debug. The most-skipped form of practice.

items.label: Kth Largest Element IN A Stream
Code k-NN classifier from scratch
interviewQuestions: Implement k-NN from scratch and explain its time/space complexity.
How would you speed up k-NN inference on millions of points without changing the prediction?

items.label: Code k-means from scratch
interviewQuestions: Implement k-means with k-means++ initialization.
Why does k-means++ initialization matter, and what does it cost vs random init?

items.label: Code logistic regression with SGD
interviewQuestions: Implement logistic regression with mini-batch SGD and L2 regularization.
What numerical-stability pitfalls do you watch out for when implementing the sigmoid + log-loss?

items.label: Code linear regression training from scratch
interviewQuestions: Implement linear regression training with vectorized gradients and a learning-rate parameter.
How do you verify your from-scratch implementation against sklearn or a closed-form OLS solution?

tracks.label: ML Coding Lab · NumPy implementation drills

items.label: Implement vectorized logistic regression with stable sigmoid and binary cross entropy
interviewQuestions: Implement fit and predict_proba for logistic regression using only NumPy.
How do you make sigmoid and log-loss numerically stable for large positive or negative logits?
What tests prove your implementation handles class imbalance, no-signal features, and all-zero rows?

items.label: Write a finite-difference gradient checker for a hand-coded model
interviewQuestions: How would you verify a hand-coded gradient without using autograd?
What epsilon do you choose for finite differences, and what breaks if it is too small or too large?
How do you debug when only one parameter group fails the gradient check?

items.label: Harden k-means for empty clusters, duplicate points, and convergence tolerance
interviewQuestions: How do you handle an empty cluster after an assignment step?
What stopping criteria would you implement beyond a fixed number of iterations?
How would you vectorize the distance computation without blowing memory on a huge dataset?

items.label: Implement confusion matrix, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC, and PR-AUC from arrays
interviewQuestions: Implement precision, recall, and F1 from labels and scores without sklearn.
How do you compute ROC-AUC by ranking scores, and why does sorting matter?
When would your PR-AUC implementation expose a bug that ROC-AUC hides?

references.label: Reddit — practical ML coding questions candidates report
